title: Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness
description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.

title: Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux
description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.

title: Type-safe daemon: eliminate invariant-checking unwraps
description:
Eliminate unwraps in daemon code by encoding proven invariants in the type system.

**Goal:** No `unwrap()` in non-test daemon paths where failures can be caused by repo state or git IO.

**Approach:** "Parse, dont validate" — move checks to where data enters the system and let types remember what has been validated.

**Subtasks:**
- Serde boundary validation for identity types
- Encapsulate DepKey (no self-loops)
- LoadedRemote proof type
- require_live helper
- with_mutation transaction helper
- Collapse live/tombstones into BeadEntry
- Remove duplicate key from DepEdge

See individual beads for design details.

title: Migration: make imported comment NoteIds globally unique
description:
## What's Wrong
Go comment notes are imported with `NoteId::new(c.id.to_string())`. If comment IDs are not globally unique across issues in beads-go exports, notes can collide and overwrite/merge unexpectedly.

## Where
- src/migrate/go_export.rs:288 (NoteId::new(c.id.to_string()))

## Why It Matters
Data loss / silent merging during migration: two different comments could end up with the same note ID.

## Suggested Fix
- Make note IDs include the issue ID (and/or created_at) e.g. `go-comment-<issue-id>-<comment-id>`.
- Add a migration test fixture with repeated comment IDs across different issues to verify uniqueness.

## Acceptance
- Import never drops/merges distinct comments due to note ID collisions.

title: Add --type flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot change a beads type after creation. Had to close bd-d37 and recreate as epic bd-2fr.

**Solution**
Add `--type` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update <id> --type=epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate type is one of: task, bug, feature, epic, chore
- Type changes should be LWW like other fields
- Consider if changing TO epic should warn if bead has no children
- Consider if changing FROM epic should warn if bead has children

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update --type <TYPE>` works
- [ ] Invalid types rejected with clear error
- [ ] Type change reflected in `bd show`

title: Smart commit messages from beads context
description:
Make sync commit messages on refs/heads/beads/store more descriptive. Instead of 'sync: +1 created', show what actually changed:

- created bd-xxx: "Title here"
- updated bd-yyy desc: "new description"  
- closed bd-zzz: "reason"
- deleted bd-abc

This helps with debugging and auditing bead changes over time. Since these commits live on a separate ref (beads/store), they won't clutter the main code history.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait
description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance
- `bd init` fails (or returns structured warning) when it cannot load the repo state.
- `SyncWait` has a single source of truth (no duplicate paths).

title: Add daemon control commands (stop, restart, status)
description: Add subcommands to 'bd daemon' for controlling the daemon: stop, restart, status. Currently have to kill the process manually.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Refactor send_request and send_request_no_autostart to share common code
description:
## What's Wrong
send_request and send_request_no_autostart in src/daemon/ipc.rs duplicate the request encoding/response parsing logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:828-838 (send_request)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:844-859 (send_request_no_autostart)

## Why It Matters
If we change the wire format or add error handling, we need to update both places.

## Suggested Fix
Extract common send/receive logic into a helper that takes a stream.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Nix flake skips tests (doCheck=false) - CI gap
description:
## What's Wrong
flake.nix has doCheck=false to avoid test failures during nix build. This means `nix build` won't catch test regressions.

## Where
- flake.nix:42-43

## Why It Matters
Users installing via nix won't get test validation. If tests break, nix users get broken builds.

## Options
1. Fix whatever causes tests to fail in nix sandbox (likely git/network related)
2. Add a separate `nix flake check` that runs tests outside the build
3. Accept the gap and rely on CI (current state)
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Write migration guide for beads-go to beads-rs
description:
Document the migration path for users of the Go version to switch to Rust. Cover:

- Data format changes (if any)
- CLI differences
- New features in beads-rs
- How to migrate existing beads data

This helps adoption and shows the project is mature enough to replace the Go version.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Persist root slug in meta.json; default new IDs to repo-name
description:
## What's Wrong
Bead IDs now support `<slug>-<suffix>` to match beads-go (repo-name slug), but new repos still default to `bd` because slug selection is inferred from existing state.

## Where
- src/daemon/executor.rs: preferred_bead_slug(...) falls back to "bd" when store is empty
- src/git/wire.rs: meta.json only stores format_version; no place to persist root slug

## Why It Matters
- Fresh repos should default to the repo-name slug (beads-go parity) or at least allow configuring it.
- Inferring slug from existing IDs fails for empty stores and is an implicit heuristic.

## Suggested Fix
- Add `root_slug` to `meta.json` (WireMeta + core Meta), and set it on init (derive from repo dir name or remote URL basename; sanitize).
- Use persisted `root_slug` for new ID generation.
- Decide compatibility strategy (format_version bump if needed; ensure old readers ignore unknown fields or gate by version).

## Acceptance
- After `bd init` in a repo named `foo`, new IDs default to `foo-...` (or a clearly documented rule).
- `bd create` in an empty store uses `meta.root_slug`, not a heuristic.

title: Daemon: remove unwraps in query/executor; enforce invariants via types
closed_reason: Superseded by epic bd-2fr - broken into individual beads

description: darin@book
title: darin@dusk
Collapse live/tombstones into single BeadEntry map
description:
**Problem**
The invariant "live ∩ tombstones = ∅" is enforced by careful method design:
```rust
pub struct CanonicalState {
    live: BTreeMap<BeadId, Bead>,
    tombstones: BTreeMap<BeadId, Tombstone>,
    // ...
}
```

This relies on `insert`/`delete` being correct. A bug could violate the invariant.

**Design**
Make the invariant structurally impossible:
```rust
pub enum BeadEntry {
    Live(Bead),
    Tombstone(Tombstone),
}

pub struct CanonicalState {
    beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>,
    deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>,
}
```

Now a bead cannot be both live and tombstoned - the type system prevents it.

**Design Notes**
- Bigger refactor than the other type improvements
- Consider doing this when touching `state.rs` for other reasons
- `get_live`/`get_tombstone` become pattern matches on entry
- Iteration patterns change (`live.values()` → `beads.values().filter_map(...)`)
- Wire format (`src/git/wire.rs`) may need adjustment

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadEntry` enum in `src/core/state.rs`
- [ ] `CanonicalState` uses single `beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>`
- [ ] All accessors (`get_live`, `get_tombstone`, iteration) updated
- [ ] Wire format handles the change
- [ ] Invariant comment updated to note its now structural
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/git/wire.rs

title: Add require_live helper to eliminate bead lookup boilerplate
Encapsulate DepKey to enforce no self-loops by construction
Remove duplicate key from DepEdge
description:
**Problem**
`DepKey` is stored both as the map key AND inside `DepEdge.key`:
```rust
deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>

pub struct DepEdge {
    pub key: DepKey,      // <-- duplicates the map key
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

This duplication means:
1. Wasted memory
2. Possible key/value mismatch bugs (though unlikely)

**Design**
Remove `key` from `DepEdge`:
```rust
pub struct DepEdge {
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

Callers that need the key get it from the map iteration:
```rust
for (key, edge) in &state.deps {
    // key is available here
}
```

**Design Notes**
- Wire format needs to handle this - on serialization, include key; on deserialization, reconstruct
- `DepEdge::new` and `DepEdge::join` signatures change (no longer take/return key)
- Check all usages of `edge.key` and ensure key is available from context

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `DepEdge` no longer contains `key` field
- [ ] Wire format correctly serializes/deserializes (key comes from map, not struct)
- [ ] All `edge.key` usages updated to use key from iteration context
- [ ] `DepEdge::new`/`join` updated
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/dep.rs, src/git/wire.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs

title: Add bd binary to nix devshell
description: The nix flake devShell should include the bd binary so hooks like 'bd prime' work inside nix develop. Currently bd is only available after building from source.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add --parent flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot reparent a bead after creation. Beads created independently cannot be linked to an epic as children later.

Currently `--parent` only works at create time:
```
bd create "subtask" --parent=bd-epic
```

**Solution**
Add `--parent` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update bd-xyz --parent=bd-epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate parent exists and is not deleted
- Should prevent circular parent relationships
- Consider `--parent=""` or `--no-parent` to unparent a bead
- Parent changes should be LWW like other fields
- ID format: beads created with --parent get hierarchical IDs (bd-abc.1). Reparenting existing beads probably keeps their original ID rather than renumbering.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update <id> --parent <epic-id>` works
- [ ] Invalid/deleted parent rejected with clear error
- [ ] Circular parent reference rejected
- [ ] Can unparent a bead (remove from epic)
- [ ] `bd show` reflects new parent
- [ ] `bd epic status` includes reparented beads

title: Add LoadedRemote proof type to eliminate repo_state unwraps
Smart commit message threshold (5) is hardcoded
description:
## What's Wrong
The threshold for switching from detailed to count-based commit messages is hardcoded to 5 in to_commit_message().

## Where
- src/git/sync.rs:92 (`if !self.details.is_empty() && total <= 5`)

## Why It Matters
Minor - the magic number 5 isn't configurable or documented. Fine for now, but if users want different behavior they can't change it.

## Suggested Fix
Either document why 5, or make it a const at module level.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Scheduler spawns unbounded threads on reschedule (threadpocalypse)
description:
**Problem**
`src/daemon/scheduler.rs` spawns a new thread on every schedule call, and reschedules do not cancel old sleepers. Under bursty ops, this devolves into "death by a thousand sleeping threads".

**Design**
Single timer mechanism:
- Keep a min-heap of `(deadline, remote)`
- State loop computes next deadline and uses `crossbeam::channel::after(next - now)` (or one dedicated timer thread)
- When rescheduling, update the heap entry (or push and ignore stale entries by checking current deadline in a map)

This makes the daemon "boring" which is good.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] At most 1 timer thread (or zero with channel-based approach)
- [ ] Rapid reschedules dont accumulate sleeping threads
- [ ] Stress test: 1000 rapid mutations, verify thread count stays bounded
- [ ] Existing sync behavior preserved (debounce still works)

**Files:** src/daemon/scheduler.rs

title: rustfmt.toml uses unstable options on stable
description:
## What's Wrong
cargo fmt prints warnings because rustfmt.toml sets unstable_features=true and group_imports=StdExternalCrate, which require nightly rustfmt.

## Where
- rustfmt.toml

## Why It Matters
Noisy output in CI/local workflows (for example, just release-patch runs cargo fmt -- --check). It can also cause formatting drift if contributors have different toolchains.

## Options
1. Remove unstable settings and rely on stable rustfmt defaults.
2. Pin nightly rustfmt in dev shell/CI to support the config.

---
Discovered while: running cargo fmt during migration slug work.

title: darin@dusk
Add with_mutation helper for borrow-checker friendly mutations
description:
**Problem**
Borrow checker forces this awkward pattern:
1. Borrow `repo_state` to check something
2. Need `&mut self` to tick the clock
3. Drop borrow, re-borrow, and `unwrap()` because "I proved it exists earlier"

**Design**
Wrap mutation in a helper that ticks before handing out the borrow:
```rust
impl Daemon {
    fn with_mutation<R>(
        &mut self,
        loaded: &LoadedRemote,  // or repo: &Path + git_tx
        f: impl FnOnce(&mut RepoState, Stamp) -> Result<R, OpError>,
    ) -> Result<R, OpError> {
        let write = self.clock_mut().tick();
        let stamp = Stamp { at: write, by: self.actor().clone() };
        let rs = self.repo_state_mut(loaded);
        f(rs, stamp)
    }
}
```

Now `apply_update`/`apply_close`/`apply_claim` can do "check + mutate" in one borrow.

**Design Notes**
- Depends on `LoadedRemote` existing (bd-xxx) OR can take repo+git_tx and call ensure_repo_loaded internally
- Consider whether stamp should be passed in or returned for callers that need it after
- May want variants: `with_mutation` vs `with_mutation_loaded` depending on whether repo already loaded

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `with_mutation` helper exists in `src/daemon/mod.rs`
- [ ] At least one executor method uses it (start with simplest case like `apply_claim`)
- [ ] Pattern eliminates the "check, drop, tick, re-fetch, unwrap" sequence
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs

title: Add serde boundary validation for identity types (BeadId, ActorId, NoteId, Priority)
Daemon restart on client version mismatch
description:
## What's Wrong
If an older `bd daemon run` process is already running, a newer `bd` CLI will connect to it and may hit confusing errors when behavior/protocol expectations change (e.g. bead ID parsing rules).

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: client connects to existing socket without verifying daemon version
- src/daemon/run.rs: daemon is spawned from current executable only when no daemon is reachable

## Why It Matters
Upgrading `bd` can silently keep using an older daemon until the socket disappears. This can break new features (like non-`bd-` id slugs) and makes failures look like user input errors.

## Suggested Fix
Add a lightweight handshake (e.g. Request::Ping returns daemon version/build hash). If mismatch with client, have client request shutdown + respawn, or refuse with a clear message.

---
Discovered while: implementing beads-go migration root slug support.

title: darin@dusk
CI docs drift: xtask/coverage references but no xtask crate
description:
## What's Wrong
Repo docs/config mention a coverage job and a `cargo xtask coverage` path, but the repo has no `xtask` crate and current workflows don't include the referenced coverage job.

## Where
- .github/AGENTS.md (mentions coverage + xtask)
- .cargo/config.toml (defines `xtask` alias)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (current actual CI)

## Why It Matters
Stale CI documentation misleads contributors and future agents. The `xtask` alias suggests functionality that doesn't exist.

## Suggested Fix
Either:
1) Add an `xtask/` crate and wire a real coverage job, or
2) Remove the alias and update .github/AGENTS.md to match reality.

## Acceptance
- CI docs accurately describe the workflows that actually run.
- No dead `cargo xtask` alias unless backed by a crate.

title: Fix collision resolution: winner can be deleted by resolution tombstone
description:
**Problem**
In `src/git/collision.rs`, collision resolution remaps the loser and writes a tombstone for the old ID using a "resolution stamp". But the merge rule in `core/state.rs` is: tombstone wins unless the beads `updated_stamp()` is strictly newer.

If the tombstone stamp is newer than the winner beads updated stamp (likely, since resolution stamp is generated "now"), **the winner gets deleted during join**. This violates spec §4.1.1 ("winner retains original id") and is catastrophic.

**Design Options**

*Option A (cleanest):* Lineage-scoped tombstones
Tombstone includes the losing beads `created` stamp (or content-hash) so it only kills that lineage, not the winner occupying the ID.

*Option B (minimal change):* Emit two stamps on collision resolution
- `t_del` for the loser-id tombstone  
- `t_win` for a no-op "bump" on the winner bead so `t_win > t_del`
This makes the winner survive the delete rule while preventing loser resurrection.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Deterministic test: collision where winner is older, loser is newer, resolution happens later
- [ ] Winner survives resolution in all timestamp orderings
- [ ] Loser cannot resurrect after resolution
- [ ] Spec compliance verified

**Files:** src/git/collision.rs, src/core/state.rs
